---
title: "AI images"
description: "Generate item and category photos with AI, or improve photos you already uploaded."
sidebarTitle: "AI images"
icon: "sparkles"
---

Not every restaurant has a photographer on call. Rumizi can generate item
and category images from the name, description, and any existing photos,
or improve a phone photo you already took.

## What you can do

<Columns cols={2}>
  <Card title="Generate an image" icon="wand-magic-sparkles">
    One tap creates an image from your item name, description, and
    category context. Use it when you don't have a photo yet.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Improve an image" icon="arrow-up-right-dots">
    Clean up a phone photo — improve lighting, remove clutter, recompose.
    Use it when you have a photo but it's not quite right.
  </Card>
</Columns>

## Where to use it

AI buttons live in two places:

- **Menu editor → Items / Categories → image slot.** Generate or improve
  the image for a single item or category.
- **Gallery.** Pick any existing gallery image and improve it in place.

## How generation works

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the item">
    In the editor, tap **Generate image** on the item card or inside the
    item dialog.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Wait a few seconds">
    Rumizi sends the item name, description, and category to an AI image
    model. Generation takes a few seconds.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Review">
    If you like it, save. If not, regenerate — each tap creates a new
    image.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
Better descriptions produce better images. An item called "Pasta" with no
description becomes a generic pasta shot; "Spaghetti cacio e pepe with
black pepper and Pecorino Romano" becomes something that looks like the
dish you actually serve.
</Tip>

## Improving an existing photo

If you already uploaded a photo but it's not great:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the image">
    In the gallery or item dialog, tap the image.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Tap Improve with AI">
    Rumizi uses the current image as a starting point and makes an
    improved version.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Compare and keep">
    You see the before and after side by side. Keep whichever one you
    prefer.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Where AI images live

Generated and improved images go to the same gallery as manually
uploaded photos. Once saved, you can re-use them anywhere — on items,
categories, or the landing page.

## Turning it off

If you'd rather not use AI images, your admin can disable the feature on
your venue. Manual upload always works regardless.
